behavior_delta_observed: First end-to-end substrate-shaped-pipeline-to-Learner-corpus round-trip succeeded today: 2 schema-conforming training_anchor blocks (LMFDB GL(3) ml_predicted + numerically_certified pair, the AA-019 trust-tier distinction made concrete) ingested into LearnerRecord JSONL with correct trust-tier-aware routing (ml_predicted → survived/conditional, numerically_certified → promoted/decidable). AA-013 anti_anchor cross-batch presence + consumer-hook fixture creation closes the doctrine-to-fixture loop ChatGPT 2026-05-13 flagged. Pipeline plumbing all functioned; only ingester adjustment needed was wrapped-vs-flat JSONL shape unwrapping (~25 LOC at ingest_training_anchors.py parse loop). The substrate-shaped pipeline thesis (substrate volume + quality compounds into Learner-corpus growth) has its first concrete instance.

behavior_delta_status._note: Per Aporia template + P2 ticket value-advancement rule. 'fixture_created' requires both ≥1 LearnerRecord landed AND AA-013 fixture exists at named path. 'eval_run' and 'eval_passed' gated on Learner head existing — paused indefinitely per pivot/strategic_pivot_2026-05-11_substrate_volume_first.md.

notes: Ingester needed a 25-LOC unwrap adjustment to accept Techne's stager output shape {block_type, payload: {...}, metadata...} in addition to the original flat {...} shape. Both code paths now live. Same change also lets the ingester filter a type-agnostic validated.jsonl by block_type==training_anchor, resolving the filename-scheme observation flagged in T-2026-05-13-ergon-to-techne-pilot-ingest-results.
Below-threshold batch (2 records) lands at under_threshold/ subdir per spec §3.2 — correct routing; not promoted to v1.0 corpus assembly without manual review.
v1.1 schema explicit-bs_coverage path remains untested at runtime; flag for next cycle.

deferred_concerns: Buchanan primitive scope: SecondCoefficientPositivityObstruction (staged in primitive_proposal.jsonl) must be scope-narrowed at Techne v4.0+ registration time. Per ChatGPT 2026-05-13 analysis: applies to POSITIVE LINKS / Jones polynomial, NOT generic knots. Without explicit scope-narrowing (e.g. applies_to: positive_links_only; condition_family: second_jones_coefficient_pm1_pm2), a downstream Learner could overreach to 'Jones second coefficient decides positivity'. Add scope_warning as a required_field at registration time. Does NOT block today's ingest.

AA-013 routing-correction surfaced at vocabulary level (primitives.md TensorRankWitness entry per Aporia e1124ec3), not within AA-013 prose itself. Template deferred_concerns[1] guidance satisfied via primitives.md path rather than AA-013-prose-edit path; both options were acceptable per the original framing.
